items.check: The output is a finished community_project_poster for public-facing use, not a notes page, extraction table, source screenshot, generic mockup, or placeholder.
items.scoring_hint: Task completion should drop clearly when required env-grounded content is largely missing, even if the image is visually polished. Placeholder language such as coming soon, unavailable, pending, details will be available, cannot finalize, or not accessible does not count as completion.

items.check: The image attempts to include the required display content requested in task.md: final public title/name, approved timing, responsible person or organization, target audience or public context, and exactly three poster-safe highlights or claims.
items.scoring_hint: Do not require identical phrasing, but the required content types must be visible. Optional details are not required.

items.check: Critical required facts are taken from the final public version rather than draft, internal, outdated, pending, or unsupported material.
items.scoring_hint: Critical fact omissions or substitutions should heavily reduce this item. Light paraphrase is acceptable when the meaning remains correct.

items.check: Major required context, including target audience or public context, is grounded in the documents and visible in the image.
items.scoring_hint: Accept concise wording, but do not credit broad generic audiences that lose the documented scope.

items.check: The image includes exactly three poster-safe highlights, claims, findings, or takeaways selected from approved public-facing material.
items.scoring_hint: Do not penalize concise paraphrase; penalize adding extra highlights or using internal/process-only notes as highlights.

items.check: Avoids old titles, old dates, unconfirmed venue, unpublished price, pending credential/award/certification, internal sponsor, draft affiliation, waitlist/internal registration, and unsupported claims when present in the env documents.
items.scoring_hint: Negative-only: award full credit when these details are absent. Penalize only explicit inclusion of prohibited details.

items.check: Optional details are not required. If optional CTA, location, or supporting context appears, it must use confirmed public-facing information rather than internal or outdated notes.
items.scoring_hint: Omission of optional details earns full credit; deduct only for included details that are wrong, internal, unsupported, or misleading.

items.check: Selects, compresses, groups, and prioritizes the long document into a clear public-facing image message rather than copying raw paragraphs, FAQ text, or internal notes.
items.scoring_hint: Structuring credit requires mostly correct core env facts; placeholder or generic content should score low even with a tidy layout.

items.check: Makes the title/name and three highlights or claims the main message, with timing, lead entity, and audience/context as supporting information.
items.scoring_hint: Reward correct emphasis and compression of grounded facts, not mere visual neatness.

items.check: Looks like a polished public poster, intro image, or advocacy visual with intentional typography, spacing, color, and composition.
items.scoring_hint: Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this item to compensate for missing or incorrect env facts.

items.check: All essential visible text is genuinely readable, non-overlapping, and high contrast at normal viewing size.
items.scoring_hint: Missing facts are not unreadable text; reduce this only when text that appears in the image is visually hard to read.

items.check: Information density is appropriate for the target image type: not overcrowded, not sparse, and not placeholder-like.
items.scoring_hint: Reward clean hierarchy and breathing room independent of factual correctness.

score_caps.reason: The output does not produce a usable public-facing target image.
Multiple core facts such as title/name, timing, or lead entity are wrong or missing.
The image explicitly includes multiple forbidden, outdated, internal, or unsupported details.
